evidence.text: â Strong brand reputation and career growth â Competitive salary & benefits â Exposure to high-impact projects and top clients â Excellent training and mentorship programs â Strong company culture and networking opportunities Prestigious Firm with Intense Work Culture & Growth Opportunities.
Great people, everyone genuinely wants to support you and your work. Great people, but hard to grow if not in the right practice

Exposure to high-impact, strategic projects across industries -Extremely smart, driven, and collaborative colleagues -Strong feedback culture with rapid skill development -Clear performance expectations and meritocratic growth -Supportive leadership and global exposure Steep learning curve with exceptional growth opportunities
The pros: excellent career development, accelerated learning curve, strong culture and network, and exposure to senior clients early. A Launchpad for Long-Term Career Growth
Great exposure and learning opportunities

Work-Life Balance: The company genuinely respects work-life balance. Thereâs a clear encouragement to log off on time, and thereâs no pressure to stay late unnecessarily. Learning Opportunities: A variety of training programs and upskilling resources are available. Employees are encouraged to continuously grow and develop professionally. Approachable HR Team: HR is accessible and responsive. One-on-one connects...
Smart colleagues, helpful for career development and learning Good for career development, but bad work-life balance
Great salary and talented co workers Exceptional salary, terrible work life balance
Like the people (working level) High salary Drastic work life balance, good salary

This is an internal role that is aligned to an industry or capability area, where you work directly with Partners and senior leaders in a range of functional roles (product, innovation, commercial, strategy & ops) to grow the 'business unit' aka industry or capability unit. It is slightly less than the client facing salary with a more manageable work life balance, you still get access to Bain consulting training...
Emphasis on learning, Focus on professional development, Prioritization of client results. Friendly and welcoming office culture. High-growth inclusive workplace
Culture is the best part. You can reach out to any Bainie from any offices for a support and will always found yourself surrounded by kind / supportive people Great culture
Really interesting casework, wonderful colleagues, solid pay, great benefits Smart, humble colleagues and fascinating work
Good company, supportive peers and collaborative environment Good
Dallas start classes are typically very large compared to other Texas offices. Great place to start out of college. + New office renovation coming within the year Good culture, large office
Very comprehensive insurance coverage with good flexible benefits Good benefits
Great benefits, health insurance is amazing Benefits
WFH Benefits , New mobile benefits, Good Benefits and Pay

Bain values its employees by providing excellent benefits, free healthcare, and opportunities for learning. The company is supportive of mental health and LGBTQ+ issues, offers a hybrid work environment, and allows flexible time off. A People focused company
Good compensation and rewards. Great benefits Good pay but bad WLB
Same as headline + brand value Good stable job
Learning curve, people, cool projects Great place to work
Food, culture, growth, supportive environment Fantastic culture

- great environment for learning - wide exposure to different industries and case types - really smart and nice people who care about your growth!! - fun and inclusive office culture :) great environment! lots of learning :)
Learning, accountability, growth, expertise and team Great school
Long hours, not substantial bonus (in line with competitors though) Great Opportunities, Long hours, good benefits
Long hours and stressful with lots of negative feedback Good culture but long hours
- Easy to get pushed out in case of underperforming - Very long hours Great culture, competitive pay but very long hours
Long hours ~40hrs/week is a minimum and can reach up to & creep into weekends Excellent Perks at cost of long hours
Long hours (60-70 hours); Potentially lot of travel and very taxing Fulfilling work with long hours
Long hours, no work life balance Good pay, long hours
You work like a dog. Prepare to have no social life Great Companyâprepare for no work life balance
- excessive work - unrealistic timelines - too much stress No work life Balance
The pay is not competitive Most people friendly company

Work ends usually after 10 PM. There is no real opportunity to take long holidays cause projects are always calling you to join. It is difficult to maintain a life outside work since you will be so tired you won't have energy to do anything else. High intensity work and low work life balance
Toxicity Politics Burnout Unfair ... Toxic Politics

Punished for breathing, for having an opinion, for speaking up. The glass ceiling for women is very visible (it's more like a concrete ceiling). Women throw female subordinates under the bus to have 'control'. Men throw their female subordinates under the bus to strengthen their ego. Don't even think about joining as an industry hire - I was told by leadership that I would always be at a disadvantages for now having...
unfair company, favoritism, no care from senior leadership for the working teams, politics, lies Not fair
Toxic workplace, senior leaders have old workingstyle Toxic workplace

Toxic management that promotes people only based on hard skills Burn-out culture (at least 3 people in my team went burn out in 1 year) Company that promotes "Bainee" culture VS individuality (you do not feel free to express yourself, dress how you would love to ecc) Workaholics, bad gossiping, I felt like I was back in 1980s - a lot of shining marketing but the reality is very bad, full of people taking leave of ab...
lots of backstabbing, very competitive, didnt feel welcome Fast Growth but very unfriendly work environment
Bain India culture is very poor compared to Bain Global stagnant growth Good to stay but can feel stagnated
sometimes you don't need so much growth... I like it so far

Hours and pressure can be hard to deal with, and could impact other areas of your life so be clear-eyed going in and know when to walk away. You may walk away after 2 or 3 years without any clearer direction of what you want to do. Bain puts you on the cases they want to put you on, so it's not a great place to discover your career calling - figure that out while you're in school. The staffing model is not what they...
Favouritism in promotions, no clear defined growth Too many perks but too much favouritsm
Keen competition with your peers, rarely any support from senior, lots of overtime working Think twice
no work lie balance, toxic work environment used to be great but falling off
If you see rapid skill development as a con whilst juggling various personal roles, then yes, this one is massive! Literally the best place to work for parents
Slow promotions and can get hierarchical Great culture
long working hours with high speed Best place to work
